message:
fix(policy): allow git push --force-with-lease as safe operation

Problem: The git force push blocking rules incorrectly caught
--force-with-lease, which is the safe variant designed to prevent
overwriting remote changes you haven't fetched.

Changes:
- Removed explicit allowed rule for --force-with-lease (unnecessary
  since it shouldn't match blocked patterns anyway).
- Fixed blocked patterns to use strict word boundaries:
  - Old: \`git\s+push\s+.*--force\` matched --force-with-lease
  - New: \`git\s+push\s+.*(\s|^)--force(\s|$)\` only matches --force alone
  - Same fix applied to -f short flag pattern.

Why --force-with-lease is safe:
- Only pushes if remote ref matches local tracking ref.
- Fails if someone else pushed since your last fetch.
- Standard workflow for rebasing feature branches.
- Recommended by Git documentation over bare --force.

Blocked (dangerous):
  git push --force origin main     # Overwrites unconditionally
  git push -f origin feature       # Same, short form

Allowed (safe):
  git push --force-with-lease      # Checks remote first
  git push --force-with-lease=origin/feature  # With explicit ref

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
